docs.text: Welcome to AutoFeatureEngineering 👋🏽 Welcome to Auto Feature Engineering library! This library makes your life way easier when you are trying to do some testing on machine learning models. You can do the feature engineering part in just one line of code! PYPI link for this page auto-fe Getting Started: Installation and One line code Advanced customizable Options Bonus Features Installation Installation steps You can install easily by pip command pip install auto-fe
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Welcome to Auto Feature Engineering library! This library makes your life way easier when you are trying to do some testing on machine learning models. You can do the feature engineering part in just one line of code! PYPI link for this page auto-fe
docs.title: Welcome to AutoFeatureEngineering 👋🏽

docs.text: Installation and One line code Advanced customizable Options Bonus Features
docs.title: Getting Started:

docs.text: Installation steps You can install easily by pip command pip install auto-fe
docs.title: Installation

docs.text: API reference One line feature engineering: import pandas as pd from AutoFeatureEnginerring import AutoFeatureEngineering data = pd . read_csv ( r "load_csv" ) # one line! out = AutoFeatureEngineering ( data ) . auto_fe () Args Description int_imputation_method Imputing numerical values. Default method is 'mean'. There are certain methods available! knnImputer or mice obj_imputation_method Imputing categorical values. Default method is 'most frequent' Return Description dataframe Imputed dataframe Customizable options: import pandas as pd from AutoFeatureEnginerring import AutoFeatureEngineering # get the data data = pd . read_csv ( r "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RAravindDS/auto_fe/main/research/train.csv" ) # impute with your own algorithm! out_df = auto_fe_obj . auto_fe ( int_imputation_method = 'KnnImputer' ) # this will impute the values with KnnImputer out_df = auto_fe_obj . auto_fe ( int_imputation_method = 'mice' ) # this will impute the values with mice imputer with default values! Bonus Methods! example import pandas as pd from AutoFeatureEnginerring import AutoFeatureEngineering # get the data data = pd . read_csv ( r "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RAravindDS/auto_fe/main/research/train.csv" ) # it will grab the numerical columns auto_fe_obj . int_columns () # It will grab the numerical columns! # it will grab the categorical columns auto_fe_obj . string_columns () # grabbing missing values based on datatype categorical_missing_col , numerical_missing_col , non_missing_col = auto_fe_obj . get_missing_values () # It will return the dataframe! # it contains missing values based on datatypes!
docs.title: API

docs.text: import pandas as pd from AutoFeatureEnginerring import AutoFeatureEngineering data = pd . read_csv ( r "load_csv" ) # one line! out = AutoFeatureEngineering ( data ) . auto_fe () Args Description int_imputation_method Imputing numerical values. Default method is 'mean'. There are certain methods available! knnImputer or mice obj_imputation_method Imputing categorical values. Default method is 'most frequent' Return Description dataframe Imputed dataframe
docs.title: One line feature engineering:

docs.text: import pandas as pd from AutoFeatureEnginerring import AutoFeatureEngineering # get the data data = pd . read_csv ( r "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RAravindDS/auto_fe/main/research/train.csv" ) # impute with your own algorithm! out_df = auto_fe_obj . auto_fe ( int_imputation_method = 'KnnImputer' ) # this will impute the values with KnnImputer out_df = auto_fe_obj . auto_fe ( int_imputation_method = 'mice' ) # this will impute the values with mice imputer with default values!
docs.title: Customizable options:

docs.text: example import pandas as pd from AutoFeatureEnginerring import AutoFeatureEngineering # get the data data = pd . read_csv ( r "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RAravindDS/auto_fe/main/research/train.csv" ) # it will grab the numerical columns auto_fe_obj . int_columns () # It will grab the numerical columns! # it will grab the categorical columns auto_fe_obj . string_columns () # grabbing missing values based on datatype categorical_missing_col , numerical_missing_col , non_missing_col = auto_fe_obj . get_missing_values () # It will return the dataframe! # it contains missing values based on datatypes!
docs.title: Bonus Methods!
